aboutText: has a broad, flattened, oval-shaped body with triangular tail fin, a reduced, dulled snout and a small mouth containing bristle-like teeth. The dorsal fin contains 14 spines and 19–21 soft rays, and the anal fin has 3 spines and 20–21 soft rays. The dorsal and anal fins both dangle behind the body. This species attains a maximum total length of 45 cm (18 in) and weight of 1,600 g (56 oz). Males may be larger than females.
distinctiveFeatures: Blue with yellow rims on scales.
Abundance: Common in Florida, Bahamas,Gulf of Mexico, Bermuda.
habitatBehavior: Swim slowly in the vicinity of sea whips, sea fans, and corals.
diverReaction: Wary, tend to keep their distance.

aboutText: Large gray disk-shaped angelfish with bright yellow pectoral fins and a yellow tail margin; common on Caribbean reef slopes.
distinctiveFeatures: Gray body with yellow pectoral fins and tail edge.
Abundance: Common on Caribbean and Florida reef slopes.
habitatBehavior: Often alone or in pairs over sponge and coral on mid-depth reefs.
diverReaction: Keeps distance; slow approaches for photos.
